title: Equal Parts of a Whole
essential_question: How can you identify and describe equal parts of a whole?
strategies: Check that all parts are the same size and shape.
Count the total number of equal parts to name the fraction.
If parts are different sizes, they are NOT equal parts.

pretest.question: A shape is divided into parts. Which word describes parts that are the same size?
pretest.explanation: Equal parts are parts that are exactly the same size.

pretest.question: A circle is cut into 2 equal parts. What are these parts called?
pretest.explanation: Two equal parts of a whole are called halves.

pretest.question: A rectangle is divided into 4 equal parts. What are these parts called?
pretest.explanation: Four equal parts of a whole are called fourths.

pretest.question: A square is divided into 3 equal parts. Which name is correct?
pretest.explanation: Three equal parts of a whole are called thirds.

pretest.question: Which of the following is NOT an equal share?
choices: A pizza cut into 8 equal slices
A sandwich cut into 2 equal halves
A cake cut into 3 unequal pieces
A ribbon cut into 4 equal pieces
pretest.explanation: Unequal pieces are not equal shares — each piece must be the same size.

learn.title: What Are Equal Parts?
learn.content: When a whole is divided into parts that are ALL the same size, we call them EQUAL PARTS. If any part is bigger or smaller than the others, the parts are UNEQUAL. Example: A sandwich cut exactly in half → 2 equal parts. A cake cut with one large piece and one small piece → unequal parts.

learn.title: Names for Equal Parts
learn.content:
The number of equal parts tells us the name:
• 2 equal parts → halves (each part = one half)
• 3 equal parts → thirds (each part = one third)
• 4 equal parts → fourths (each part = one fourth)
• 6 equal parts → sixths (each part = one sixth)
• 8 equal parts → eighths (each part = one eighth)

learn.title: Equal Parts Must Cover the Whole
learn.content: Equal parts must: (1) all be the same size, AND (2) fit together to make the whole with no gaps or overlaps. Example: A square divided into 4 equal triangles — each triangle is one fourth. All 4 triangles together make the whole square.

try.question: A rectangle is divided into 6 equal parts. What are these parts called?
try.explanation: Six equal parts of a whole are called sixths.
hints: Count the equal parts: there are 6.

feedback.correct: Correct! 6 equal parts are called sixths.
feedback.incorrect: Not quite. Count the parts — 6 equal parts are called sixths.

try.question:
Which shape shows equal parts?
A) A circle with one large part and one small part
B) A square divided into 4 same-size parts
C) A rectangle with 3 different-sized sections
D) A triangle split unevenly
choices: A circle with one large part and one small part
A square divided into 4 same-size parts
try.explanation: A square divided into 4 same-size parts shows equal parts — each part is the same size.
hints: Equal parts must all be the same size.
Look for the shape where every part is identical.

feedback.correct: Correct! Equal parts are all the same size.
feedback.incorrect: Not quite. Equal parts must all be the same size — only choice B shows this.

try.question: A pie is cut into 8 equal slices. What are these parts called? (Write the name.)
try.explanation: 8 equal parts of a whole are called eighths.
hints: Count the equal parts: there are 8.

feedback.correct: Correct! 8 equal parts are called eighths.
feedback.incorrect: Not quite. 8 equal parts are called eighths.

try.question: A square is cut into 4 parts. Three parts are small and one part is large. Are these equal parts?
choices: No, because the parts are different sizes
Yes, because they cover the whole square
No, because squares cannot be divided
try.explanation: Equal parts must all be the same size. If one part is larger, the parts are unequal.
hints: Check: are all parts the same size?
Equal means same size — if any part is bigger or smaller, they are unequal.

feedback.correct: Correct! Equal parts must all be the same size.
feedback.incorrect: Not quite. Equal parts means SAME size — these parts are different sizes, so they are unequal.

try.question: A sandwich is cut into 3 equal pieces. Ben eats one piece. What fraction of the sandwich did Ben eat?
try.explanation: 3 equal parts → thirds. Ben ate 1 of 3 equal parts = 1/3.
hints: How many equal parts is the sandwich cut into?
3 equal parts = thirds. Ben ate 1 part = 1 third = 1/3.

feedback.correct: Correct! 3 equal parts = thirds, so Ben ate 1/3.
feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The sandwich is cut into 3 equal parts (thirds). Ben ate 1 part = 1/3.

practice_r1.question: A shape is divided into 2 equal parts. What are these parts called?
hints: 2 equal parts have a special name.

practice_r1.question: A rectangle is divided into 4 equal parts. What fraction is each part?
practice_r1.explanation: 4 equal parts → each part is 1/4 (one fourth).

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. 4 equal parts → each part = 1/4.

practice_r1.question: Which word describes 8 equal parts of a whole?
A circle is cut into equal parts. Each part is called 'one sixth.' How many equal parts are there?
A square is divided into parts. Two parts are large and two parts are small. Are these equal parts?
choices: No, because the parts are not the same size
Yes, because they cover the whole
No, because squares must have 4 equal parts
practice_r1.explanation: Equal parts must all be the same size. Large and small parts are unequal.
hints: Check if all parts are the same size.

feedback.correct: Correct! Equal parts must all be the same size.
feedback.incorrect: Not quite. Equal parts must be the SAME size.

practice_r1.question: 4 friends share a pizza equally. What fraction does each friend get?

feedback.correct: Correct! 4 friends share equally → each gets 1/4.

practice_r1.question: Which is true about equal parts of a whole?
choices: They must all be the same size
There must always be 4 of them
practice_r1.explanation: Equal parts must all be the same size, no matter what shape or how many parts.

feedback.correct: Correct! Equal parts must all be the same size.
feedback.incorrect: Not quite. Equal parts must all be the same size.

practice_r2.question: A garden is divided into 6 equal sections. Each section is planted with a different flower. What fraction of the garden is each section?
Lisa says her brownie is cut into thirds. How many equal pieces does she have?
hints: Thirds tells you how many parts.

practice_r2.question: A rectangle is divided into 8 equal parts. Sam colors 1 part. What fraction is colored?
practice_r2.explanation: 8 equal parts → each = 1/8. Sam colored 1 part = 1/8.

practice_r2.question:
Which shape below shows halves?
A) A circle cut into 3 equal parts
B) A square cut into 2 equal parts
C) A rectangle cut into 4 equal parts
D) A triangle cut into 6 equal parts
choices: A circle cut into 3 equal parts
A square cut into 2 equal parts
A rectangle cut into 4 equal parts
A triangle cut into 6 equal parts
practice_r2.explanation: Halves = 2 equal parts. A square cut into 2 equal parts shows halves.

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. Halves = 2 equal parts — look for the shape with 2 equal parts.

practice_r3.question: A square is cut into 4 triangles of equal size by drawing both diagonals. Are these equal parts? What fraction is each triangle?
choices: No — triangles cannot be equal parts of a square
Yes — each triangle is 1/4 of the square
Yes — each triangle is 1/2 of the square
No — the parts must be rectangles
practice_r3.explanation: Equal parts don't have to be rectangles. 4 equal triangles each represent 1/4 of the square.
hints: Are all 4 triangles the same size?

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. 4 equal triangles are equal parts of the square, each = 1/4.

practice_r3.question: Jake cuts a sandwich into 3 pieces. He says each piece is 'one third.' His friend says the pieces are not thirds because they look different shapes. Who is correct?
choices: Jake — equal parts can be different shapes if they are the same size
His friend — equal parts must look exactly the same
Both are wrong — you cannot cut a sandwich into thirds
practice_r3.explanation: Equal parts must be the same SIZE, not necessarily the same shape. As long as each piece is the same amount, they are equal parts.
hints: Equal parts = same size, not necessarily same shape.
Think: can a circle and a triangle both be 1/4 of a shape?

feedback.correct: Correct! Equal parts must be same size — shape can differ.
feedback.incorrect: Not quite. Equal parts means same SIZE. Shape can be different.

practice_r3.question: A rectangle is divided into 8 equal parts. Amy shades 1 part. Ben shades 1 part of the same rectangle that is divided into 4 equal parts. Whose shaded part is larger?
practice_r3.explanation: When a whole is divided into MORE equal parts, each part is SMALLER. 1/4 > 1/8 because fourths are bigger pieces than eighths.
hints: More equal parts → smaller each part.
Compare: 1/4 vs 1/8. Which denominator is smaller?

feedback.correct: Correct! Fewer parts = bigger each part. 1/4 > 1/8.
feedback.incorrect: Not quite. More parts = smaller pieces. 8 parts are smaller than 4 parts, so 1/8 < 1/4.
